target.name: Mammoth Biosciences
target.founder: Jennifer Doudna, Trevor Martin, Janice Chen, Lucas Harrington

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $600M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $3.1B - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Mammoth Biosciences, a Series D biotech company founded by Jennifer Doudna and co-founders, has raised $600M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector, with direct comparables including Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B, Series C), Abridge ($5.3B, Series E), and New Limit ($3.1B, Series C).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, using the $600M raised as a proxy base, yielding $900M. Peer-Median triangulation across the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median of $5.3B, with a range of $3.1B to $10.3B. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied; while the Biotech & Health sector median trades at 6.9x P/S across the three public tickers, Mammoth's revenue is not disclosed.

Synthesizing the two applicable methods yields a valuation range of $765M (low) to $6.1B (high), with a median implied value of $3.1B. The wide band reflects stage and capital-efficiency dispersion within the peer set. Key uncertainties include whether Mammoth's technology platform and IP moat support valuations toward the peer median or upper quartile, and the timing and structure of the next financing round.
